patterns.what_to_look_for: OS command injection: user input flowing into a shell-interpreted exec sink. The danger sign is a string built from a request param passed to a shell, OR an argument that can break out of a non-shell exec via metacharacters/path. Look for unescaped concatenation reaching the command string.
source_indicators: child_process.exec/execSync with a template string containing req.body/req.query/req.params
os.system / subprocess.* with shell=True and an f-string or % concatenation
Runtime.exec(String) or ProcessBuilder with a single concatenated command string
Go exec.Command("sh","-c", userVar) or exec.Command("bash","-c", ...)
PHP system()/exec()/shell_exec()/passthru()/proc_open with request data not via escapeshellarg
user value reaching ping/curl/ffmpeg/imagemagick/git/tar wrapper without arg-array separation
backtick or popen() invocation interpolating a filename/hostname from input
blackbox_indicators: time-delay differential: payload `;sleep 7` or `|sleep 7` or `$(sleep 7)` adds ~7s to response
param values like hostname/filename/format/cmd/exec/jobid that look like CLI wrapper inputs
error output leaking shell syntax (sh: 1: ..., command not found, /bin/sh)
feature is an obvious shell wrapper (ping host, DNS lookup, archive extract, image convert)
OOB DNS/HTTP callback fires from `;nslookup <token>.collab` or `$(curl <token>)` style probe
evidence_required: source-to-sink path: tainted request field -> shell exec call with no escaping/allowlist
the exact exec API and whether a shell is invoked (shell=True / sh -c / single-string exec)
blackbox: reproducible timing delta or OOB callback tied to an injected separator, two trials
confirmation that input is not constrained to an enum/numeric/allowlist before the sink
false_positive_checks: input passed as a separate argv element (array form, no shell) -> metacharacters inert, not injectable
value validated against a strict allowlist/regex (e.g. ^[a-zA-Z0-9.]+$) or cast to int before sink
escapeshellarg/shlex.quote/proper quoting applied -> demote
the 'command' is a constant and the variable is only an unreachable/dead branch
timing delta caused by upstream network latency, not the injected sleep (control trial without payload)
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Send a benign time-delay probe (e.g. value `127.0.0.1;sleep 9` vs baseline `127.0.0.1`) and compare response latency across two trials each.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as OS command injection (CWE-78); cite the sink, the proven timing/OOB differential, and whether a shell was actually spawned.

patterns.what_to_look_for: NoSQL operator/JSON injection: untyped request body or query merged directly into a MongoDB/Mongoose/other document-store filter, letting an attacker smuggle operators ($ne, $gt, $regex, $where) or change query shape. Classic in auth: { user, pass } from req.body passed straight to findOne.
source_indicators: collection.find/findOne(req.body) or (req.query) with no per-field type coercion
Mongoose model.find({ field: req.query.x }) where x can be an object
$where with a JS string built from input, or mapReduce/aggregate $function user code
JSON body parsed then spread into a filter (...req.body) for find/update/delete
no express body type guard; querystring lib that yields objects for key[$ne]=... syntax
Knex/Sequelize raw JSON operators or qb.whereRaw with interpolated input on a JSON column
blackbox_indicators: login bypass when sending {"user":"admin","pass":{"$ne":null}} as JSON body
param style url like field[$ne]= or field[$gt]= accepted and altering result set size
Content-Type application/json endpoint where a value object yields more/auth'd records
$regex injection: {"$regex":"^a"} changes returned rows, enabling blind extraction
boolean-differential: object-typed operator value flips response between 200/401 or row counts
evidence_required: source path: request field reaching the query object without scalar/type validation
blackbox: a differential where an operator-bearing object changes auth result or row set vs scalar baseline
the datastore + query API (find/aggregate/$where) confirming operator interpretation
absence of input typing (e.g. String(x) coercion, schema cast, or strict body validation)
false_positive_checks: fields explicitly coerced with String()/Number() or validated by a schema (Joi/zod/Mongoose cast) before query
body parser configured to reject nested objects / query strictly typed -> operators can't arrive
sanitize middleware present (express-mongo-sanitize) stripping $-keys and dots
endpoint already requires verified auth on a different field, so $ne bypass yields nothing
the differential is explained by legitimate matching, not operator interpretation (re-test with literal)
patterns.suggested_validation_task: On a JSON endpoint, compare a scalar value vs an operator object (e.g. {"$ne":null} or {"$gt":""}) in one non-credential field and observe whether the result set/response state changes.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as NoSQL injection (CWE-943); show the operator-object differential and the unvalidated source-to-query path.

patterns.what_to_look_for: Server-side template injection (SSTI): user input concatenated into a template string that is then compiled/rendered, rather than passed as a data binding. The tell is input building the template source itself (render_template_string(user), Twig createTemplate(user), `${...}` in a JS template engine) which exposes the template language's expression evaluator and often RCE.
source_indicators: Jinja2 render_template_string(user) or Template(user).render(), Flask flash/format with input
Twig $twig->createTemplate($userString) or _self / array template from request
Nunjucks/Handlebars/EJS/Pug compiling a template string sourced from input (ejs.render(userSrc))
Velocity/Freemarker/Thymeleaf with inline expression from input; Spring SpEL parser.parseExpression(input)
string interpolation INTO template source: `"Hello " + name + "!"` then compiled as template
mail/notification templates where subject/body template is admin- or user-supplied and rendered
blackbox_indicators: math probe renders: input {{7*7}} or ${7*7} or #{7*7} comes back as 49
engine fingerprint differential: {{7*'7'}} -> 7777777 (Jinja) vs error (Twig)
user-controlled name/subject/label/template field reflected after server-side processing
${} or #{} or {{}} sequences in input cause 500s referencing a template engine class
reflected value where a literal stays literal but an expression evaluates
evidence_required: source path: input reaching a compile/render-of-template-string call (not a data context binding)
blackbox: an arithmetic/string expression that evaluates server-side (e.g. {{7*7}}->49), distinct from literal echo
identification of the template engine (controls exploitability/RCE reachability)
proof it is template-source injection, not autoescaped data interpolation
false_positive_checks: input is passed as a render CONTEXT variable (render_template(file, name=user)) -> data binding, autoescaped, not SSTI
engine has sandbox/autoescape on and expression returns escaped literal {{7*7}} unchanged
the 49 came from the app's own arithmetic, not template eval (test with {{6*7}} to confirm it tracks)
reflected XSS only (client-side) with no server-side evaluation -> different class, route to XSS catalog
expression syntax is stripped/encoded before reaching renderer
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Submit a harmless arithmetic expression in the suspect field ({{7*7}}, ${7*7}, #{7*7}, or {{6*7}}) and check whether the response contains the computed product instead of the literal.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as server-side template injection (CWE-1336/CWE-94); name the engine and show the evaluated expression, noting RCE reachability.

patterns.what_to_look_for: LDAP / directory-filter injection: user input concatenated into an LDAP search filter or DN without escaping, letting an attacker inject filter metacharacters ( ) * | & = to alter the query, enable auth bypass, or blind attribute enumeration. Common in login and user-lookup features against AD/OpenLDAP.
source_indicators: filter strings built by concatenation: "(uid=" + user + ")" or f"(&(uid={user})(pass={pwd}))"
ldapjs/ldap3/python-ldap/Spring LdapTemplate.search with an interpolated filter argument
DN built from input: "cn="+name+",ou=..." without escapeDN/escape_dn_chars
no use of escapeLDAPSearchFilter / ldap.filter.escape / RFC4515 escaping helper
JNDI/DirContext.search(base, filter, ...) where filter embeds raw request data
blackbox_indicators: auth/login form where payload `*` or `*)(uid=*` or `admin)(&)` changes the result
wildcard `*` in a username/search field returns all/extra records
injecting `)` or `(` triggers an LDAP filter/parse error (e.g. javax.naming, invalid filter)
boolean-differential via `*)(|(uid=*` style payloads flipping match/no-match
search box that maps to directory attributes (uid, cn, mail) and reacts to filter metacharacters
evidence_required: source path: input concatenated into an LDAP filter/DN with no RFC4515/RFC4514 escaping
blackbox: a filter-metacharacter differential (e.g. `*` widening results or `)` causing a filter error)
the LDAP API and whether an escaping helper is applied before the sink
confirmation input is not constrained to a safe charset/allowlist pre-filter
false_positive_checks: escapeLDAPSearchFilter / ldap.filter.escape / encodeForLDAP applied to every interpolated value -> demote
parameterized/bound search where placeholders are escaped by the library
input restricted to a strict allowlist (e.g. numeric id) before building the filter
the `*` result change is a legitimate substring feature, not injection (test `)` to confirm metachar parsing)
lookup hits a non-LDAP store; the filter syntax is unrelated
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Enter a single `*` and then a lone `)` into the directory-search/login field and observe result-set widening or an LDAP filter parse error versus a normal value.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as LDAP injection (CWE-90); cite the unescaped filter/DN construction and the metacharacter differential (auth-bypass or enumeration impact).

patterns.what_to_look_for: HTTP header / CRLF injection: user input written into a response header, redirect Location, Set-Cookie, or an outbound email header without stripping CR/LF. Permits response splitting, header injection, cookie setting, cache poisoning, or SMTP header injection. Also covers reflected Host/forwarded headers trusted for URL building.
source_indicators: res.setHeader/header()/addHeader with a value containing req.* and no \r\n stripping
redirect(req.query.url) / Location header from input; res.redirect with user-supplied target
Set-Cookie value built from input; raw header write in a low-level HTTP/CGI handler
email send where To/Subject/From/Reply-To header is set from request input (PHPMailer addCustomHeader, raw mail() headers)
logging or response built by concatenating input into a header line without encoding
Host/X-Forwarded-Host header used to build absolute URLs (password-reset links, redirects)
blackbox_indicators: payload with encoded CRLF (%0d%0a) in a reflected param injects a new header (e.g. Set-Cookie:inj=1) into the response
open-redirect-style param (url, next, return, redirect, dest) reflected into Location
%0d%0aHeader: value appears as a real response header, not body text
Host header poisoning: spoofed Host reflected into a generated link or cache key
email feature where header field accepts newline and adds Bcc/extra headers
evidence_required: source path: input written into a header/redirect/cookie/email-header sink with no CR/LF removal
blackbox: a CRLF payload producing a NEW header line (response splitting) or a controlled redirect target
which sink (HTTP response header vs Location vs Set-Cookie vs SMTP header) and runtime behavior
confirmation the runtime/framework does not already reject CR/LF in header values
false_positive_checks: framework/runtime rejects or strips CR/LF in header values (Node http throws on \n; modern servers block) -> not exploitable
redirect target validated against an allowlist of hosts/paths before being set
the %0d%0a is reflected into the response BODY (HTML), not a header -> XSS class, not CRLF/splitting
value is URL-encoded by the framework so CRLF stays inert
email library that enforces single-line headers / rejects newlines
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Send a benign CRLF probe (e.g. param value `x%0d%0aX-Inj-Test:1`) and inspect raw response headers for a new X-Inj-Test header, or test an off-site value in a redirect param for an unvalidated Location.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as HTTP header/CRLF injection (CWE-93/CWE-113); show the injected header line or controlled redirect and name the sink.

patterns.what_to_look_for: XML external entity (XXE) injection: an XML parser that resolves DOCTYPE/external entities while processing user-supplied XML (uploads, SOAP, SAML, SVG, DOCX/XLSX, RSS). Enables local file read, SSRF, and blind OOB exfiltration. The tell is a parser constructed without disabling DTDs/external entities.
source_indicators: Java: DocumentBuilderFactory/SAXParserFactory/XMLInputFactory without disallow-doctype-decl / external-general-entities=false
Python: lxml etree.parse with resolve_entities default, or xml.dom/pulldom without defusedxml
PHP: libxml_disable_entity_loader not set / simplexml_load_string on user XML (pre-8.0)
.NET XmlDocument/XmlReader with DtdProcessing=Parse and XmlResolver set
Node: libxmljs parse with noent:true, or a SOAP/SAML lib resolving entities
file-upload or API consuming XML/SVG/DOCX/SAML where the parser config is default
blackbox_indicators: XML/SVG/DOCX upload or XML/SOAP endpoint accepts a DOCTYPE and reflects entity expansion
in-band: a defined entity referencing a benign value is expanded in the response
OOB: parameter/external entity pointing at an attacker URL triggers a server callback (DNS/HTTP)
Content-Type application/xml or text/xml endpoint that processes the body server-side
error referencing entity/DTD resolution (e.g. failed to load external entity, DOCTYPE not allowed)
evidence_required: source path: user XML reaching a parser whose DTD/external-entity resolution is not disabled
blackbox: an entity expansion observed in-band, OR an OOB callback from an external-entity probe
the parser/library and its security configuration flags
confirmation the endpoint actually parses the XML server-side (not just stores it)
false_positive_checks: parser hardened: DOCTYPE disallowed / external entities disabled / defusedxml / XmlResolver=null -> not vulnerable
library/runtime version that disables external entities by default (e.g. PHP 8+, modern .NET) with no override
the XML is never parsed (stored/forwarded as opaque bytes) -> no sink
entity expansion blocked but error is generic -> confirm with an OOB probe before claiming
schema validation rejects DOCTYPE before entity resolution
patterns.suggested_validation_task: Submit XML with a benign external entity pointing to an OOB collaborator URL (parameter entity, no local-file read) and watch for a DNS/HTTP callback; do not exfiltrate sensitive files.
patterns.reporting_guidance: Report as XXE (CWE-611); show the OOB callback or in-band entity expansion and cite the parser's unsafe configuration.
